item.title: 美国和伊朗暂停袭击，但对下一步谈判步骤存在分歧
item.description:
DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — The United States and Iran on Monday separately announced they will send delegations to Qatar this week, though Tehran insisted it has not agreed to meet with the U.S. “at any level” afterattacks across the Persian Gulf over the weekendchallenged negotiations to end the war.
President Donald Trump said the Islamic Republic had requested a meeting with U.S. counterparts and that they planned to convene Tuesday in Dohar, Qatar.
But one of Iran’s senior negotiators denied talks had been scheduled. And the spokesman for Iran’s Foreign Ministry said Tehran was sending its delegation to Qatar, a key mediator in the negotiations, to discuss terms of the interim deal without involving the U.S.
The U.S. president has tried to preserve a fragile interim deal, but hostilities mounted in recent days in the Strait of Hormuz, through which a fifth of the world’s oil had been shipped before war began. After four days of trading strikes, both sides appeared to pause their attacks Monday.
The U.S. and Iranagreed to an interim dealearlier this month that calls for Tehran to dilute its stockpile of enriched uranium. It also waives U.S.-backedsanctions on the country, opens the Strait of Hormuz and gives each side 60 days to hammer out broader agreements.
After Trump said Monday morning on social media that the U.S. and Iran planned to meet, White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt told Fox News’ “Fox & Friends” that special envoy Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner, the president’s son-in-law, were flying to Qatar.
Pakistan, also a key mediator, had said talks between Iran and the U.S. would resume Tuesday.
But Kazem Gharibabadi, a senior negotiator for Iran, said in comments published by Iranian state media that no talks had been confirmed. And Esmail Baghaei, spokesman for Iran’s Foreign Ministry, said that its delegation was traveling to Qatar this week to discuss the planned release of frozen Iranian assets and other issues related to the deal.
“There are no negotiation meetings with the U.S. side at any level scheduled in the coming days,” Baghaei said. “The fact that U.S. representatives are traveling to Qatar has no connection with the visit of the Iranian delegation.”
During the war that began Feb. 28, Iran’s attacks and threats stopped cargo ships and tankers from moving through the Strait of Hormuz, creating a global energy crisis.
In recent days, Iran has twice attacked vessels in the strait — including a tanker filled with Qatari crude — following efforts to open Oman’s territorial waters to both inbound and outbound traffic from the Persian Gulf.
The attacks drew retaliatory American airstrikes and raised concerns that negotiations to reach a formal end to the war could be disrupted. Iran launched drone and missile attacks targetingBahrainandKuwaiton Sunday.
The strait has long been considered an international waterway despite its location in Iran and Oman’s territorial waters.
The Trump administration was operating Monday on the understanding that the U.S. and Iran are standing down after the recent back-and-forth strikes and that vessels can move freely through the Strait of Hormuz, said a U.S. official who requested anonymity to discuss the sensitive negotiations.
The U.S. official also said that Qatar planned to release $6 billion in frozen Iranian assets that would be used to purchase U.S. food products for the Iranian people.
Iranian PresidentMasoud Pezeshkianhad announced the expected release of funds earlier Monday in comments published by the state-run IRNA news agency. He called it “a great victory for the Iranian people.”
Pezeshkian, a reformist within Iran’s theocracy, is the highest-ranking official within Iran to reference the release of the funds held by Qatar, a key mediator along with Pakistan in the negotiations.
Oman’s foreign minister, Badr Albusaidi, said Monday that Oman and Iran are considering charging service-related fees for commercial ships transiting the Strait of Hormuz.
Albusaidi said services could include water safety measures, pollution prevention, navigational assistance and preparedness for incidents such as fires. He told Radio Monte Carlo while visiting France that there are “successful examples” of similar services being provided in compliance with maritime law.
Albusaidi said Oman does not support imposing transit fees on ships.
“This is internationally forbidden,” he said, “and we are abiding by these rules.”
An Iranian official warned France against “provocations” Monday after French President Emanuel Macron posted on X that France and others were coordinating efforts to clear mines from the Strait of Hormuz.
Kazem Gharibabadi, an Iranian deputy foreign minister, said on X that under the interim deal “demining is carried out solely by Iran and by no other country.”
Macron’s post came after he greeted Sultan Haitham bin Tariq of Oman ahead of high-level diplomatic talks in Paris.
Lebanese President Joseph Aoun said Monday that Lebanon is determined to deploy troops along its entire southern border as part of a framework agreement with Israel signed Friday. He made the remark while meeting with Adm. Brad Cooper, the top U.S. military commander in the Middle East.
The deal was rejected by the Iran-backed Hezbollah militant group, which triggered the latest war with Israel on March 2 when it fired rockets across Lebanon’s southern border and into northern Israel.
The Israel-Lebanon deal calls for Hezbollah to be disarmed before Israel will withdraw its troops from southern Lebanon. Israel agreed to withdraw initially from a couple of “pilot zones” where the Lebanese army would then deploy, but no details have been shared about how that will work in practice.
Hezbollah officials have warned that attempts to implement the plan could lead to civil war.
Boak reported from Washington. Associated Press writers Nasser Karimi and Amir Vahdat in Tehran, Iran; Bassem Mroue and Sally Abou AlJoud in Beirut and Russ Bynum in Savannah, Georgia, contributed to this report.

item.title: 欧洲创纪录的高温让巴黎的太平间不堪重负，让许多家庭陷入困境
item.description:
巴黎（美联社）——每隔几分钟，太平间主人的电话就会响起。自从一场破纪录的热浪开始夺走巴黎及其他地区的生命和尸体储存空间以来，给他打电话的葬礼承办人和哀悼家庭大多都有同样的问题：你们还有地方再容纳一个吗？
当他的冷藏室中的 32 个位置都被占用时，Zouhaeir Hertelli 不得不一遍又一遍地轻轻地说“不”。
“我们正面临着一个真正灾难性的局面，”他说。“我接到了数百个电话。”
本周末，随着历史性的热浪将致命的气温向东转移到欧洲其他地区，法国开始计算其造成的人员损失。
周一，法国首都的气温达到 44 摄氏度（111.2 华氏度），巴黎人和游客试图通过寻求阴凉和涌入装有空调的博物馆来避暑。法国正咬紧牙关，迎接一周破纪录的气温，白天气温高达 40 摄氏度（104 华氏度）以上，晚上则令人难以入睡的大汗淋漓。（美联社视频由奥列格·塞蒂尼奇拍摄）
统计与高温相关的死亡人数的统计和公共卫生工作可能需要数周或数月的时间。但已经很明显的是，法国是第一个从 6 月中旬开始遭受高温天气影响的国家，在法国，严酷无情的极端气温所造成的死亡人数非常惨重，尤其是在家中死亡的老年人。
赫尔泰利说：“由于热浪，我们正在应对死亡人数激增的情况，我们真的已经吃饱了，吃饱了，吃饱了。”
该国家公共卫生机构在其第一份初步估计中表示，上周法国热浪最高峰期间，死亡人数激增，欧洲最大国家的大部分地区都被烤焦了，许多地方的气温飙升至 40 摄氏度（104 华氏度）以上，并且还打破了夜间高温记录——对于疲劳的身体来说，这是一场令人筋疲力尽的二击。
法国公共卫生部门表示，上周三，法国迎来有史以来最热的一天，有超过 1,200 人死亡，打破了前一天创下的纪录。相比之下，4月和5月热浪前的死亡率约为每天900至1000人。
报告称，周四有超过 1,400 人死亡，周五又有 1,400 人死亡。
该机构警告说，随着更多在家中和老年人护理机构死亡的人收到更多死亡证明，预计在这炎热的三天里，至少会有 1,000 人死亡，而在这些机构中，大多数死亡仍未以电子方式登记。
该机构表示：“因此，死亡率将高于最初的数字。”
报告称，在其研究的三天内，迄今为止登记的死亡人数中有 85% 涉及 65 岁及以上的人，而且在家中死亡的人数急剧增加，增加了约 40%，尤其是在巴黎地区。
赫尔泰利和殡葬行业的其他人士表示，巴黎太平间的存储空间很快就耗尽了。市政厅表示，为市政太平间安装了两个临时存储单元，每个存储单元可容纳 20 个位置，市医院还额外提供了 50 个位置。
不过，赫尔泰利表示，他采访过的殡葬承办人告诉他，他们不得不将尸体存放在距离巴黎 80 公里（50 英里）远的沙特尔以及首都周边的其他地区。他说，为了腾出更多空间，他已请求当局允许在他的太平间外临时安装冷藏集装箱，该太平间位于巴黎奥利机场旁边，但仍在等待绿灯。
“家庭正在遭受痛苦，”他说。“我们无法为他们提供解决方案，因为殡仪馆已经满了。所以我们深受影响，我们对他们表示同情，但我们无能为力。我们确实面临着一个问题，一个大问题，”他说。
2003 年的历史性高温被认为造成了 15,000 人死亡，这引发了全国范围内对老年人护理问题的思考，老年人受到的影响尤其严重。去年异常炎热的夏季也导致超过 5,700 人死亡。
巴黎葬礼承办人维罗妮克·贝特朗 (Véronique Bertrand) 表示，她担心人们已经忘记了教训。
伯特兰说：“我们目前处理的大多数死亡病例都是独居在家、与世隔绝的人。考虑到他们被发现时的情况，除了这些是由高温造成的死亡之外，没有其他结论。”
“我认为人们绝对需要醒悟，团结需要回归，2003年发生的事情导致了朝这个方向的运动，人们想到他们的邻居，想到周围那些独居的人，也许时不时地检查一下他们是否在喝水，是否受到照顾，”她说。
“随着时间的流逝，我们可能已经忘记了这种情况可能会再次发生，而且情况甚至可能会更糟。”

item.title: 余震让灾区救援人员焦躁不安，委内瑞拉人搜寻更多地震废墟
item.description:
LA GUAIRA, Venezuela (AP) — With the window for finding survivors shrinking fast, Venezuelans combed Monday through more ruins of buildings toppled by last week’s powerfulback-to-back earthquakes, and a 4.6 magnitude aftershock rumbled through the disaster zone in the northern state of La Guaira.
Relief organizations say the first 72 hours after a natural disaster is the most crucial time period for rescues, though survival can be extended if people have access to food and water. Five days after the twin quakes struck northern Venezuela, attention turned to the humanitarian crisis that was taking shape in devastated regions.
Residents watch rescuers’ efforts to reach survivors beneath the rubble of a building that collapsed in the earthquakes in La Guaira, Venezuela, early Monday, June 29, 2026. (AP Photo/Matias Delacroix)
The death toll stood at more than 1,700 people, according to the government.
Major questions loomed about whether the cash-strapped government under acting President Delcy Rodríguez — who came to power in January after the Trump administration seized former President Nicolás Maduro — will be able to coordinate the effort needed to care for thousands of people who have been left homeless.
Facing criticism that authorities have done too little, too slowly, the government has promoted its rescue efforts on social and state-run media. On Monday, it shared footage of Rodríguez inspecting a school-turned-shelter for displaced people in the hard-hit northern town of Catia La Mar and survivors being lifted out of the ruins to applause.
But such bright spots are rare at the quake’s epicenter, where families keep vigil at search sites.
“We have to stay strong, even without food, without sleep,” said Ana Rada, watching as civil defense workers looked for her brother. “Until I see the body, I still have hope.”
Following a weekend of smaller aftershocks, Monday’s temblor struck near the epicenter of last week’s quakes — 27 kilometers (17 miles) north of Caraballeda on Venezuela’s Caribbean coast — and measured 4.6 magnitude, according to the United States Geological Survey. Colombia’s geological survey put the magnitude at 5.1.
Jorge Rodríguez, the leader of the Venezuelan National Assembly, said there were no immediate reports of additional damage, but the aftershock sent residents in the capital of Caracas screaming into the streets.
AP correspondent Karen Chammas reports on an aftershock in Venezuela five days after twin powerful earthquakes rocked the north of the country.
“Here we are again, back in the street. I don’t know when we’ll have a moment of true peace,” said Concepción Hernández, 51, who evacuated her apartment building in the Chacao municipality of Caracas.
Volunteer Jean Sosa, who joined rescue teams searching for earthquake survivors, explains what he saw after going under the rubble of a building where rescuers are trying to reach trapped people in La Guaira, Venezuela, Monday, June 29, 2026. (AP Photo/Matias Delacroix)
The Caracas Metro said it would temporarily suspend service Monday to inspect infrastructure following the aftershock.
The disaster has raised expectations for the Trump administration, considering its takeover of Venezuela’s oil industry earlier this year.
In a briefing with reporters, a senior State Department official said 300 first responders sent from the U.S. are working on the ground — alongside dozens of other international rescue teams — and two dozen C-17 military transport planes arrive every day with supplies. Financial support from the U.S. now exceeds $300 million.
The American military is also assisting with some repairs, including damage to the port in La Guaira to enable the arrival of more relief supplies by sea. Another team is helping to manage air traffic after the quakes destroyed part of the control tower at Simón Bolívar International Airport in Caracas, said the official, who spoke on condition of anonymity because he was not authorized to speak publicly.
It seemed unlikely, however, that the Trump administration would grant temporary legal protections to Venezuelans as previous administrations have done for people from disaster-stricken countries already in the U.S. Such action was taken after earthquakes in 2010 in Haiti and 2001 in El Salvador.
Rescue workers search through the rubble of a building that collapsed when earthquakes struck La Guaira, Venezuela, Sunday, June 28, 2026. (AP Photo/Matias Delacroix)
Venezuelans have been a major focus of the Trump administration’s immigration crackdown, with officials revoking temporary legal protections for hundreds of thousands of Venezuelans and stepping up deportation flights.
Among the rescuers digging through the rubble Monday in La Guaira was miner Jean Sosa, who said he was deported from the U.S. in January over a missed immigration court hearing and returned to Caracas last month, dazed by an odyssey that he said began in shackles at an Arizona immigration detention center. The journey involved traveling by bus through five countries after immigration agents left him in southern Mexico without his passport, phone or wallet.
A rescuer carries a girl pulled from the rubble four days after twin earthquakes struck La Guaira, Venezuela, Sunday, June 28, 2026. (AP Photo/Pedro Mattey)
Since arriving Wednesday in La Guaira to visit family and friends, Sosa has raced to pull people from the rubble in the absence of national rescue teams.
“I’m not involved in politics, but I believe many people could have been saved if there had been equipment and support from top authorities from the very beginning,” he told The Associated Press, wearing a helmet and a black T-shirt splotched with dust in the port city where he said he had already rescued 20 people alive.
Those rescues heartened him, he said, and gave him hope for more despite the lack of supplies. “We’re working without gloves, without equipment, borrowing supplies, improvising bandages and whatever else we can.”
The full scale of the damage remained unclear. Jorge Rodríguez, who is the brother of the acting president, said that as of Monday, a total of 15,866 people had been affected, while the number of damaged or collapsed buildings had reached 855.
A preliminary assessment by NASA estimated that the earthquake damaged or destroyed 58,870 buildings. The assessment relied on radar imagery from the European Space Agency’s Sentinel-1 satellites, which can detect changes to infrastructure.
The United Nations has said that up to 6.8 million of Venezuela’s nearly 30 million residents may be affected, which could mean being displaced or losing access to essential services such as electricity and water.
Because of the chaos and poor cellphone service, many Venezuelans have turned to non-governmental digital databases to report their loved ones as missing. More than 50,000 people were reported missing on one such database, though it is unclear how many have been found.
DeBre reported from Buenos Aires, Argentina. Associated Press writers Jorge Rueda and Regina Garcia Cano in Caracas, Venezuela, and Gabriela Aoun Angueira in Tijuana, Mexico, contributed to this report.

item.title: 在囚犯制服狱警数小时后，当局结束了对北卡罗来纳州监狱的接管
item.description:
北卡罗来纳州温莎（美联社）——周一早些时候，囚犯们制服了惩教人员，占领了北卡罗来纳州东部一所监狱的部分区域，但几小时后，当执法人员进入监狱并控制住监狱时，围困结束。
北卡罗来纳州调查局在社交媒体上发布的一份声明中称，凌晨 5 点左右开始接管温莎伯蒂-马丁地区拘留中心，当时有 3 名警卫和 88 名囚犯在里面，当地、州和联邦当局立即做出反应。
国家调查局在一份声明中表示，到下午早些时候，调查局和联邦调查局已经“清理了该设施”。“所有囚犯和工作人员都很安全，受伤的人也已接受治疗。”
调查局表示，在评估损失期间，该设施将保持安全，囚犯已被转移到其他设施。温莎位于罗利以东约 120 英里（190 公里）处。
囚犯们俘虏了两名警卫，第三名警卫逃脱了。伯蒂县警长泰隆·鲁芬在新闻发布会上表示，经过谈判，两名警卫和 80 名囚犯被释放，里面只剩下 8 名囚犯。
鲁芬说，被释放的两名警卫正在接受治疗，但他没有透露他们受伤的详细信息。
鲁芬在一份声明中表示，这对公众没有构成威胁。
当局没有解释为什么在接管时只有三名警卫看守监狱。
鲁芬没有说明此次收购的原因。
“现在我们正在努力控制很多事情，”他说。“我会尽快向公众发布这些信息。”

item.title: 亚历克斯·默多 (Alex Murdaugh) 重返法庭，听取谋杀案重审，他的真实犯罪传奇仍在继续
item.description:
南卡罗来纳州列克星敦（美联社）——亚历克斯·默多（Alex Murdaugh）周一因谋杀妻子和儿子的指控而重返法庭，他默默地出席了预审听证会，听证会内容大多缺乏实质内容，但随着真正的犯罪感继续令人着迷，这场听证会内容很丰富。
南卡罗来纳州最高法院上个月推翻了默多的谋杀罪和终身监禁判决。周一，一位新法官制定了听证会时间表，将重审定于 4 月 5 日开始。她还确定了确保辩方和检方交换证据的最后期限，这一过程称为证据开示。
从国际机构、当地电视台到播客，数十家媒体聚集在拥有 200 人的列克星敦县法院内，再次记录了这位曾经富有且威风凛凛的南方律师的每一次额头摩擦和疑惑的眼神。
“我看到我们座无虚席，”法官黛布拉·麦卡斯林在听证会开始时说道。
对于许多人来说，这是一次难得的机会，让我们看到州立监狱的生活如何改变了 58 岁的默多。在承认从客户及其家族律师事务所盗窃约 1200 万美元的罪名后，他正在服刑 40 年联邦刑期，同时又被判处 27 年州刑。
与法庭上的其他人不同，法官说她对这个故事很陌生，这个故事结合了可怕的双重谋杀和强大的法律王朝的垮台。
“我对第一次审判一无所知，所以当你告诉我一些事情时，请完整地告诉我，”麦卡斯林告诉律师。
检察官称，默多枪杀了他的妻子玛吉和 22 岁的小儿子保罗，因为他相信对他们的死亡表示同情将为他赢得时间来解决他的金融犯罪问题。2021 年的那个时候，他差点被他的律师事务所和一名青少年的家人曝光，这名青少年在保罗喝酒时撞船后提起了非正常死亡诉讼。
陪审团在 2023 年判定默多犯有两项谋杀罪。虽然他承认自己是小偷、保险骗子、糟糕的律师和长期的阿片类药物成瘾者，但他坚决否认自己犯有谋杀罪。
默多周一穿着橙色监狱连身裤，紧闭着嘴听着。
有一次，当辩护律师迪克·哈普特利安 (Dick Harpootlian) 要求法官允许默多在法庭上穿便服时，他告诉他的当事人起立。
哈波特连指出：“手上戴着铁链，腰上戴着铁链，脚上也戴着铁链。”他表示，当默多只被判犯有金融犯罪时，陪审团会认为他像危险的罪犯一样被镣铐束缚。
检察官克雷顿·沃特斯表示，被监禁的被告佩戴约束装置和连身裤很重要。“每次有人被移出法庭，都会带来安全风险。”
辩护律师希望在法律纠纷中被取消律师资格的默多在监狱里能够使用一台没有互联网的笔记本电脑，这样他的团队就不必打印并向他提供证据。Harpootlian 周一表示，有超过 20,000 页的文件。
“哈普特连先生，他在第一次审判前肯定复习过那些内容，不是吗？”法官问道。
另一项审前动议要求检察官将默多妻子指甲下发现的 DNA 移交给私人实验室进行检测。调查人员表示，这封信来自一名身份不明且无关的男子。辩方表示他们将承担测试费用。
“我会让你付出代价的，”法官打趣道，引来法庭上的一阵笑声。
在交谈过程中，默多做了个鬼脸，咬着下唇。
辩方还希望在科尔顿县以外举行下一次审判，科尔顿县是凶杀案发生地和第一次审判的所在地。这件事周一尚未决定。
调查人员和纸上谈兵的侦探都花了几个小时仔细研究不在场证明、时间线和数字线索，其中包括检察官称破获此案的一段手机视频。他们声称，在视频中可以听到默多的声音，该视频是他的儿子在家族庞大财产的狗舍发生枪击事件前不久拍摄的。默多最初声称他当时正在睡觉。
在第一次审判中，一些陪审员表示，负责在审判期间监督证据和陪审团的科尔顿县法院书记员告诉他们，在默多为自己辩护作证时要注意他的肢体语言，不要被他可能说的话所愚弄、困惑或迷惑。
州最高法院裁定默多有罪，并推翻了他的定罪。
法官们还担心，关于默多如何从客户那里偷窃的证词太多，其中许多客户都陷入了困境。

item.title: 最高法院任期即将结束，有关特朗普权力的重大案件有待裁决
item.description:
华盛顿（美联社）——最高法院即将结束这一任期，该任期的重点是唐纳德·特朗普总统对总统权力的广泛主张。
特朗普限制与生俱来的公民权、随意解雇大多数独立机构负责人以及罢免现任美联储理事等，是法官预计将于周一开始裁决的其余八起案件之一。
在西弗吉尼亚州和爱达荷州的案件中，法院还在权衡是否遵守大约一半州禁止跨性别女孩和妇女参加公立学校和大学体育运动的法律。
两起与选举相关的案件仍然存在，涉及州法律允许接收邮寄选票的宽限期（前提是选票在选举日之前发送），以及限制政党支持国会和总统候选人的支出。
同样突出的是关于地理围栏搜查令的争议，地理围栏搜查令收集手机用户的位置历史记录以寻找犯罪现场附近的人。批评者称这种做法是一种侵犯公民自由的捕鱼行为。
到目前为止，最高法院的保守派多数大多接受特朗普的移民镇压政策，包括上周的一项决定，允许政府终止对因祖国战争或自然灾害而来到美国的人的临时法律保护。另一项决定可能会使逃离迫害的人更难在美国寻求庇护。
在四月份的辩论中，法官们对特朗普的行政命令表示了更加怀疑的态度，该命令将推翻长期以来达成的谅解，并拒绝向非法或暂时居留在美国的父母所生的孩子授予公民身份。
法院还驳回了特朗普关于根据紧急权力法单方面征收大范围关税的主张。
二月份的决定引起了特朗普的愤怒，包括对他任命的两名法官尼尔·戈萨奇和艾米·科尼·巴雷特进行了异常严厉的个人谴责，他们投票反对他。
去年 12 月，特朗普解雇独立机构成员的权力范围是历史最悠久的悬而未决的案件。法官们似乎有可能推翻或大幅缩小一项已有 91 年历史的裁决。在总统罢免参议院确认的官员之前，需要有一个理由，比如玩忽职守。
结果似乎毫无疑问，因为保守派允许解雇在案件审理期间生效，即使下级法院法官发现解雇非法。
法院似乎不太愿意支持特朗普因抵押贷款欺诈指控立即解雇美联储理事丽莎库克的提议，但她否认了这一点。在美联储 112 年的历史中，还没有哪位总统解雇过美联储理事。
按照惯例，法院在 7 月 4 日之前完成工作。本周之后，下一次公开会议是 10 月的第一个星期一。

item.title: 根据新的民意调查，美国人如何看待国家的成就和在世界上的地位
item.description:
WASHINGTON (AP) — Americans have grown less proud of their country’s history or the way its democracy works over the past decade, according to a new AP-NORC poll.
Americans’ pride in the U.S. on several key attributes has dropped since 2017 — including the nation’s military and its political influence around the globe — according to the survey fromThe Associated Press-NORC Center for Public Affairs Research. This poll was conducted in April, as the United States and Iranfought over the Strait of Hormuzin a prolonged war that started with the U.S. and Israel launching strikes on Iran.
NewGallup pollingalso finds that only 53% of U.S. adults are “extremely” or “very” proud to be an American, the lowest reading in the trend dating back to 2001.
The findings point to a broad decline in patriotic sentiment over a tumultuous period that included most of President Donald Trump’s first term, the COVID-19 pandemic and rising inflation that contributed to a backlash against President Joe Biden. That timeframe also covers Trump’s return to the White House, where he’s taken more aggressive actions on immigration and issues abroad.
Much of the falling positivity comes from Democrats, who have become increasingly disenchanted with the country since Trump’s first term.
At the same time, most U.S. adults say that being an American is “extremely” or “very” important to their identity, highlighting an enduring connection, even as some become increasingly critical of the country’s past or the government’s current actions.
Americans’ pride in the way democracy works in the U.S. has declined 14 percentage points, falling from 42% in February 2017 to 28% now.
In addition, Americans’ pride in their armed forces has dropped 19 percentage points since 2017, and pride in the U.S.’s history has declined 14 percentage points. In each case, the drop is largely driven by Democrats, with some movement among independents as well.
Karla Galdamez — a 48-year-old Democrat who used to teach U.S. history — believes America has regressed under the Trump administration. While the Californian is not proud of Trump, she is pleased with how far the U.S. has come in 250 years.
“It’s a country that really wanted to be different and really wanted to be better,” she said. “Despite some of the very ugly history that we have of segregation and slavery ... if you look at the trajectory of the last 250 years, we’ve done nothing but get better and move toward a more egalitarian nation.”
Only 14% of Democrats and 28% of independents say they are “extremely” proud to be an American, according to Gallup’s new poll, compared with 70% of Republicans.
The AP-NORC poll found that Republicans are especially likely to be proud of the nation’s armed forces. About 9 in 10 Republicans say the military makes them “extremely” or “very” proud, compared with about 6 in 10 U.S. adults.
Samantha Fulks, a 40-year-old in San Antonio, Texas, says she’s proud to be an American and doesn’t hide it. The Texas Republican showcases that pride with an American flag in her front yard — as well as Trump flags in the back yard — and she plans to wear red, white and blue on the Fourth of July. Fulks comes from a military family, and while she believes the country’s involvement in Iran is unnecessary, she remains a proud supporter of the military.
“I still support our troops no matter what they do,” Fulks said.
Matt Stafford, a 39-year-old in Massachusetts, is proud of being an American, even if the U.S. political system frustrates him.
He has a bald eagle tattooed on his back to represent the United States, its freedoms and “all the things we’re supposed to stand for as a country.” But despite that national pride, he often finds himself frustrated by politicians on both sides. Stafford — a centrist who identifies as “politically homeless” — wants Democrats and Republicans to come together to look out for their constituents in middle America.
“I love America, but our biggest problem is how we’re pushing both sides — like the left and the right — to the extremes,” he said.
For many Americans, their partisanship is often intertwined with their national identity. The poll finds that Republicans are much likelier than Democrats or independents to say being an American is “extremely” or “very” important to their personal identity.
Younger people are also much less likely than older people to say being an American is highly important to their personal identity. About three-quarters of Americans ages 60 and older say being an American is highly important to them, compared with only about one-third of U.S. adults under 30.
The AP-NORC survey found that the vast majority of Black Americans — 73% — say their race or ethnicity is “extremely” or “very” important to how they see themselves, higher than the share that say that about being an American.
Vincent Harris, a 60-year-old in California, says his identity as a Black man rises above other attributes for him because of how Black men are treated in America.
“A lot of people are scared of Black men just because we are Black and we are male. And that’s crazy,” Harris said. “People don’t even take you for who you are as a person; they just look at your race.”
About half of Hispanic Americans say their race or ethnicity is highly important to them, compared with 22% of white Americans.
Black and Hispanic adults are also more likely than white adults to say their family’s ancestry or country of origin is highly important to their personal identity.
Harris, who identifies as a gay man, says being an American is “a wonderful thing” because of the freedoms that Americans have, despite the obstacles he’s had to overcome.
“It’s great to be an American — regardless of race, gender, sexual orientation, religion, or whatever. As long as you have that freedom of choice as an American, that’s a great thing,” Harris said. “Right now, I wouldn’t live in any other country in the world. I’m here. I love it.”
The AP-NORC poll of 2,596 adults was conducted April 16-20 using a sample drawn from NORC’s probability-based AmeriSpeak Panel, which is designed to be representative of the U.S. population. The margin of sampling error for adults overall is plus or minus 2.6 percentage points.

item.title: 最高法院驳回特朗普在 E. Jean Carroll 性虐待案中推翻 500 万美元判决的主张
item.description:
华盛顿（美联社）——最高法院周一驳回了唐纳德·特朗普总统提出的推翻陪审团 500 万美元裁决的请求，即他对作家进行性虐待。让·卡罗拉 (Jean Carrollat​​) 于 20 世纪 90 年代中期在纽约市一家百货公司任职，后来诽谤了她。
正如往常一样，高等法院以一份简短且未解释的命令拒绝受理此案。没有明显的异议。他的律师表示，特朗普还计划对第二次诽谤审判后另一陪审团裁定卡罗尔获得 8330 万美元赔偿金的判决提出上诉。
这一决定是在法院提交其最大意见之际做出的，其中包括一项扩大他对除美联储之外的联邦官僚机构的解雇权的裁决。
特朗普在社交媒体帖子中称转交卡罗尔案的决定“令人惊讶”，并表示他将继续与诽谤指控作斗争。“这个案件确实是针对美利坚合众国及其所代表的一切，”他写道。
美联社驻华盛顿记者 Sagar Meghani 报道称，最高法院驳回了特朗普总统提出的驳回针对他的 500 万美元性侵犯判决的请求。
特朗普的律师辩称，导致判决的指控是由“高度煽动性”的证据裁决支撑的，其中包括允许另外两名在几十年前指控特朗普性虐待的女性作证的裁决。特朗普否认了所有三名女性的指控。
特朗普的律师辩称，法官在此案中违反了联邦证据规则。他们认为这是对特朗普作为总统独特职责的干扰，尽管这一判决是在他返回白宫之前做出的。
“这种对总统的虐待行为不能继续存在，”律师贾斯汀·D·史密斯在法庭文件中写道。共和党人特朗普此后提名史密斯担任上诉法院法官。他的律师在周一的一份声明中称此案为“自由法律”。
卡罗尔的律师敦促法官审理此案。他们认为，这些妇女的证词是相关的，因为指控相似，而且刘易斯·卡普兰法官的决定与全国各地的其他人的决定一致。“这个问题不值得审查，”与法官没有关系的律师罗伯塔·卡普兰写道。
她在周一的一份声明中表示，周一的决定确认陪审团的裁决将维持有效。她说：“他对判决提出上诉的多次努力都失败了，今天的裁决结束了他逃避对其行为承担责任的努力。”
长期担任咨询专栏作家、前电视脱口秀主持人的卡罗尔在 2023 年的一次审判中作证称，特朗普将 1996 年春天的一次友好会面变成了曼哈顿特朗普大厦街对面的奢侈品零售商波道夫·古德曼 (Bergdorf Goodman) 更衣室中的暴力袭击。陪审团还认定特朗普在 2022 年否认卡罗尔的指控时对诽谤卡罗尔负有责任。
美联社不会透露那些自称遭受性侵犯的人的身份，除非他们像卡罗尔那样公开挺身而出。
特朗普成功抵御了其他重大法院判决，包括纽约上诉法院驳回的纽约民事欺诈罚款超过 5 亿美元。最高法院还于 2024 年授予他广泛的刑事起诉豁免权，尽管后来勉强拒绝了他在纽约封口费案中停止判刑的请求。
美联社驻纽约撰稿人詹妮弗·佩尔茨和驻华盛顿的米歇尔·普莱斯为本报告做出了贡献。
请关注美联社对美国最高法院的报道：https://apnews.com/hub/us-supreme-court。

item.title: 韩国教练因提前退出世界杯引发总统严厉批评而辞职
item.description:
韩国队主教练洪明甫周日辞职，此前该队在世界杯上的快速出局遭到了该国总统的严厉谴责，总统称该教练“无能”，并下令对国家队计划进行全面审查。
韩国队在首场比赛中战胜了捷克队，然后在接下来的两场 A 组比赛中输给了南非和墨西哥。
韩国希望能够作为今年扩大赛的第三名球队之一晋级下一轮。周六，刚果队以 3-1 击败乌兹别克斯坦队，比赛结束。
一支有望晋级的球队突然退出，导致国家队计划陷入混乱，并遭到了总统李在明的严厉斥责。他对教练提出了尖锐的批评。
总统说：“作为一名前职业足球俱乐部名誉主席，并且本质上是红魔队的一员，我对这一意外结果不仅感到惊讶，而且深感困惑。”
总统首先批评了国家队的结构和监督以及教练的任命。
总统说：“事实再次证明，人事决定决定一切。如果忠诚和派系重于能力，而任命一个无能的人为领导人，结果就像火一样可以预见。”
57岁的洪在球队离开墨西哥大本营之前宣布辞职，并向韩国球迷道歉，韩国队在那里打完了全部三场小组赛。
这是洪第二次入选国家队。他是2014年巴西世界杯的主教练，当时韩国队也未能小组赛出线。
“对于所有热爱和支持我们国家队的韩国人民，我想真诚地道歉。今天，我想宣布我将辞去国家队主教练的职务，”洪说。
“作为主教练，任何解释都不能取代最终的结果。我没能带来我们的人民所期望的结果。所有的责任都在我身上。”洪说。“今天，我辞去了国家队主教练的职务。但是，我对韩国足球的心不会改变。我现在将支持韩国国家队重新获得我们人民的信任和喜爱。”
韩国是世界杯的常客，连续11次参加世界杯，并在2002年共同主办世界杯时进入半决赛。
李呼吁国家文化体育旅游部对国家队及其失败进行审查。
李说：“对于这种荒谬的情况造成的深切失望，我真诚地向公众道歉。我们将迅速采取行动改革体育管理，以确保这种情况不再发生。”

item.title: 世界杯淘汰赛阶段：有些球队的道路比其他球队更容易
item.description:
MIAMI GARDENS, Fla. (AP) — If theWorld Cupwas the NCAA basketball tournament, then teams like Morocco and the Netherlands would have some serious anger toward the selection and seeding committee right now. England, Mexico, Portugal and Spain might not be all that happy, either.
Lionel Messi and Argentina, on the other hand, probably wouldn’t complain much about their potential path to the World Cup final four.
The bracket is set for the knockout stage of the World Cup. There was no selection committee; slots were predetermined — Group A winner on this line, Group D runner-up on this line, etc. — so it wasn’t exactly like how the NCAA tournaments go. And FIFA doesn’t reseed like some sports, so a couple lower seeds are certain to get into at least the Round of 16.
So, when taking the 32 qualifiers for the knockout stage and ranking them like it’s an NCAA tournament — essentially seeding the field 1 to 32 based on the FIFA live rankings entering Sunday — it’s easy to see why some “regions” might be tougher to navigate than others.
— Quarterfinal: July 9 at Foxborough, Massachusetts.
— Round of 32 matchups: No. 12 Germany vs. No. 27 Paraguay, No. 2 France vs. No. 26 Sweden, No. 29 South Africa vs. No. 25 Canada (won 1-0 by Canada on Sunday), No. 7 Netherlands vs. No. 6 Morocco.
— Outlook: Netherlands vs. Morocco in the Round of 32 means that at least one of the seven highest-ranked teams left in the tournament won’t even get to the Round of 16. France and Germany — perennial European powers — could meet in the Round of 16. And Canada (the first team through to the Round of 16 after winning the knockout stage opener over South Africa on Sunday) might have to change time zones twice on its way to the quarterfinals, while Germany, Paraguay, France and Sweden will all be in either Massachusetts, New Jersey or Pennsylvania in the Rounds of 32 and 16.
— If the seeds hold: France would play Morocco in the quarterfinal.
— Quarterfinal: July 10 at Inglewood, California.
— Round of 32 matchups: No. 8 Portugal vs. No. 13 Croatia, No. 3 Spain vs. No. 18 Austria, No. 14 United States vs. No. 30 Bosnia and Herzegovina, No. 10 Belgium vs. No. 17 Senegal.
— Outlook: The U.S. gets one of the lowest-ranked teams left in the tournament to open the Round of 32 and would remain in the Pacific time zone until the semifinals. Cristiano Ronaldo and Portugal did not get an easy draw at all, with Croatia right off the bat and then potentially Spain in the Round of 16. (Can’t imagine Spain is too thrilled with this, either.) Belgium vs. Senegal is in Seattle, and the winner will stay there to play the U.S. match winner; that’s a big break for weary legs.
— If the seeds hold: Spain would play Belgium in the quarterfinal.
— Quarterfinal: July 11 at Miami Gardens, Florida.
— Round of 32 matchups: No. 5 Brazil vs. No. 16 Japan, No. 24 Ivory Coast vs. No. 19 Norway, No. 9 Mexico vs. No. 20 Ecuador, No. 4 England vs. No. 28 Congo.
— Outlook: Mexico and England will be heavily favored in their Round of 32 matches, then would be in line to go head-to-head in the Round of 16 — in what would be a road game at Mexico City for England. And Mexico is one of only two teams not to surrender a goal in the group stage, with Spain being the other. Brazil gets a bit of an easier path to the quarterfinals, opening with Japan and then — if it wins — drawing the Ivory Coast-Norway winner.
— If the seeds hold: Brazil would play England in the quarterfinal.
— Quarterfinal: July 11 at Kansas City, Missouri.
— Round of 32 matchups: No. 1 Argentina vs. No. 31 Cape Verde, No. 22 Australia vs. No. 21 Egypt, No. 15 Switzerland vs. No. 23 Algeria, No. 11 Colombia vs. No. 32 Ghana.
— Outlook: Again, there is no “selection committee,” but if there was it would have gotten this one right by putting the best goalscorer in Argentina’s Lionel Messi against the tournament’s best story in Cape Verde in the Round of 32. And Messi gets to be at home in Miami Gardens, a few miles from Inter Miami’s stadium for that matchup. Australia or Egypt awaits the Argentina match winner, Switzerland-Algeria has potential for a lot of goals, and Colombia faces a Ghana team with nothing to lose.
— If the seeds hold: Argentina would play Colombia in the quarterfinal.
News and notes about the Round of 32 matchups:
— France, Mexico and Argentina were the only teams to escape group play with 3-0-0 records.
— There are four Round of 32 matchups between unbeaten teams: Netherlands (2-0-1) vs. Morocco (2-0-1), Belgium (1-0-2) vs. Senegal (1-0-2), Brazil (2-0-1) vs. Japan (1-0-2) and Argentina (3-0-0) vs. Cape Verde (0-0-3).
— Expect some goals in France vs. Sweden (combined 17 goals in the group stage), Netherlands-Morocco (16), Belgium-Senegal (14), Brazil-Japan (14) and U.S.-Bosnia and Herzegovina (13).
— On the flip side, the first goal of Mexico-Ecuador might be that match’s decider. Mexico wasn’t scored on in group play and Ecuador surrendered only two goals in those three matches.
— The semifinal matchups are the winner of the Foxborough quarterfinal against the winner of the Inglewood quarterfinal (at Arlington, Texas on July 14) and the winner of the Miami Gardens quarterfinal against the winner of the Kansas City quarterfinal (at Atlanta on July 15). The final is at East Rutherford, New Jersey on July 19.
See more of AP’s World Cup coverage here.

item.title: 对于沙特阿拉伯和卡塔尔等海湾国家来说，金钱买不到世界杯的成功
item.description:
ATLANTA (AP) — Gulf countries like Saudi Arabia and Qatar that poured a fortune into soccer and infrastructure experienced unceremonious exits from theWorld Cup, proving that big money doesn’t guarantee success on the sport’s biggest stage.
Saudi Arabia is out at the earliest point. Again. Bottom of a group that includedtiny Cape Verde, the third-smallest nation ever to compete on this stage and into the knockouts for the first time.
By signing the likes of Cristiano Ronaldo, Neymar and Karim Benzema as part of aspectacular recruitment drivein recent years, Saudi Arabia has been a major disruptor of club soccer. But on the international stage it still has a long way to go eight years out from hosting the World Cup in 2034.
On Sunday, Saudi Arabian Football Federation president Yasser Al-Misehal resigned. Taking full responsibility for the failed World Cup campaign, he said that stepping aside will allow for a “new phase” of leadership within Saudi football.
Qatar, the host four years ago, is also on its way home after just three games, so too are the other gulf nationsIranand Iraq. Compared to the success of African teams at this World Cup, with nine out of 10 advancing to the round of 32, gulf nations are struggling mightily to make their mark.
A goalless draw against Cape Verde ended Saudi Arabia’s hopes of advancing from the group phase for the first time since 1994.
“It was not what we wanted because when playing in such a match against a team that is more or less the same level as us, our performance was not good. So this gives rise to concern,” Saudi coach Georgios Donis said.
Qatar made history with its first point at a World Cup, scoring a dramatic late equalizer against Switzerland. But it was another disappointingly early exit after it waseliminated just two games into its home tournamentin 2022.
“I think that they show that at least we were able to compete in these kind of matches,” coach Julen Lopetegui said.
The appointment of Lopetegui — the former Spain and Real Madrid coach — is evidence of the type of investment Qatar has made to try to boost its performance on the global stage. Unlike Saudi Arabia, it has not embarked on such an audacious drive to attract aging stars from Europe to its domestic league.
Despite a population of 3 million people and only around 300,000 citizens, it has managed to develop enough homegrown players towin back-to-back Asian Cupsin recent years and assert its dominance on a region that includes powers like Japan and South Korea.
But it has not been able to translate those performances to the World Cup, and such an early exit marks a disappointment less than four years after hosting the tournament and spending billions of dollars to create eight state-of-the-art stadiums.
“You compare with other countries ... for sure we know who we are,” said Lopetegui. “But at the same time I think that this is one little country but with a big passion, a big investment ... we have to improve every day and they did this.
“We look to the future being optimistic about this for sure.”
It’s all about the future for Saudi Arabia, too, after winning the right to host the World Cup in 2034.
It has been on a mission to wield influence in sports around the world, from buying Premier League Newcastle to launching LIV Golf and hosting world title boxing matches and Formula 1.
The World Cup would be its standout achievement as it looks tomove away from its heavy reliance on oiland explore other revenue-generating sectors.
It will want its national team to make a statement at its home tournament and while superstar signings like Ronaldo have raised the profile of its league, the hope is that they will also raise standards.
Yet after pulling off one of thebiggest upsets in World Cup historyby beating eventual champion Argentina four years ago, there was no standout moment this time — failing to advance beyond the group phase for the sixth time in a row.
“When we have these stars in the Arabian League, I think that the more competitive the competition, the better our players will be,” said Donis. “But it’s different when we’re playing for the national team because in the national team, these experiences, there needs to be a certain mentality.”
Focus on development of homegrown talent is clear as 2034 approaches.
Star signings from overseas have slowed and some big names, including Neymar, have departed. U.S. Soccer’s sporting director Matt Crocker waslured awayto head up talent development in Saudi Arabia and youth investment is said to have doubled over the past three years.
If Saudi Arabia and Qatar have been disruptors with their sudden mega spending, Iran has been competing in World Cups since 1978.
It had to contend with difficulties regarding preparation and travel in the wake of war with the United States and only narrowly missed out on advancing as a best third place team after three draws. In seven appearances at the World Cup it has never gone beyond the groups.
Likewise for Iraq in its two appearances 40 years apart.
At a time when a supersized 48-team World Cup has opportunities for the likes of Cape Verde and Congo to make history, gulf nations are still waiting for their moment.
James Robson is athttps://x.com/jamesalanrobson

item.title: 明星制作人克莱夫·戴维斯在布鲁斯·斯普林斯汀、迪昂·沃里克等人的葬礼上受到表彰
item.description:
纽约（美联社）——肯尼·G（Kenny G）演奏了悲伤的萨克斯独奏，詹妮弗·哈德森（Jennifer Hudson）的声音高亢，布鲁斯·斯普林斯汀（Bruce Springsteen）在周一的音乐传奇人物克莱夫·戴维斯（Clive Davis）的葬礼上热情洋溢地讲话，流行音乐皇室向一位为他们的职业生涯提供了如此多支持的人致敬。
出席仪式的其他音乐明星包括迪翁·沃里克 (Dionne Warwick)、巴里·曼尼洛 (Barry Manilow)、艾丽西亚·凯斯 (Alicia Keys)、贾·鲁尔 (Ja Rule) 和史蒂维·旺德 (Stevie Wonder)。其他名人包括前众议院议长南希·佩洛西、阿德里安·布罗迪、霍达·科特布和盖尔·金。
拉比安吉拉·沃尼克·布赫达尔表示，戴维斯会对投票率感到兴奋。“克莱夫会喜欢这个，”她说。“如果家里挤满了人，他会很感动，也会因为超级巨星前来向他致敬而感到兴奋。”
该仪式不对公众开放，但在曼哈顿中央犹太教堂进行了现场直播。
戴维斯是一位唱片公司律师，后来成为音乐界最有影响力的人物之一，开启了众多明星的职业生涯，包括惠特尼·休斯顿、斯普林斯汀、凯斯和肯尼·基，并影响了卡洛斯·桑塔纳、贾尼斯·乔普林和感恩而死乐队等其他人。
斯普林斯汀称他高大、夸夸其谈、勇敢。“他生来就是为了管理一切，”老板说。他记得 1972 年，他 22 岁时遇见了他，当时他的焦虑感不断加剧。“我迫不及待地想听到你的声音，”戴维斯告诉他。试镜后，这位高管简单地说：“欢迎来到哥伦比亚唱片公司。”
“这几句话，他永远改变了我的生活，”斯普林斯汀说。
戴维斯于 6 月 22 日在曼哈顿的公寓中去世，享年 94 岁，几周前他因上呼吸道疾病住院。
布赫达尔问戴维斯最欣赏哪一首不是他参与创作的歌曲，并被告知“彩虹之上的某处”，由作曲家哈罗德·阿伦和作词家 E.Y. 创作。“叶”哈伯格于 1938 年拍摄电影《绿野仙踪》。布赫达尔随后演唱了一首高亢版本。“索尼音乐经典就是你的家，”索尼音乐娱乐公司首席执行官罗布·斯金格开玩笑说。
沃里克讲述了 20 世纪 70 年代末戴维斯如何敦促她与曼尼洛合作的故事，但她最初拒绝了。但戴维斯的建议卓有成效：他们的合作专辑《Dionne》获得了白金销量，并获得了两项格莱美奖。“所以那天巴里和我成为了非常非常好的朋友，”她笑着说道。
曼尼洛回忆道，戴维斯敦促他录制由斯科特·英格利希和理查德·科尔创作的摇滚歌曲“白兰地”。曼尼洛将其变成一首情歌并为戴维斯演奏。“就这么做吧，”戴维斯告诉他。他们将其重新命名为“曼迪”。它排名第一。“他从一开始就相信我，”曼尼洛说。
哈德森演唱了伦纳德·科恩的《哈利路亚》，然后当她转向休斯顿的热门歌曲《我将永远爱你》时，她变得情绪化。“我们爱你，克莱夫”，她在获得全场起立鼓掌之前说道。
虽然许多唱片公司高管发现自己的影响力随着年龄的增长而减弱，但戴维斯的影响力似乎在增长。他为艾瑞莎·富兰克林 (Aretha Franklin) 和桑塔纳 (Santana) 等知名艺术家的职业生涯注入了新的活力，并帮助凯斯 (Keys) 和几位早期的“美国偶像”获奖者 (包括凯莉·克拉克森 (Kelly Clarkson)) 开启了职业生涯。
斯普林斯汀表示，随着戴维斯的去世，世界已经消亡——唱片公司高管领导的自上而下的变革者，其中还包括贝里·戈迪、艾哈迈德·厄特根、莫·奥斯汀和杰里·韦克斯勒等有远见的人：“他们定义、热爱和维持了唱片业。”
斯普林斯汀说：“每一天，我都会坐在我的大房子里的大前廊上，周围都是我的大汽车和我的大家庭，俯瞰着我的大院子，克莱夫不会在我的大脑顶部吹口哨。”
当凯斯走近讲台并借了一块手帕时，她泪流满面。“我实际上不是一个爱哭的人。我处在一个陌生的地方，”她对人群说道。她回忆起15岁时第一次见到戴维斯时，她因为迟到而跑步，并在钢琴上为他弹奏歌曲。“你在我身上看到了一些我刚刚开始在自己身上看到的东西，”她在给戴维斯的信中写道。
“在一个经常将艺术简化为商业、将天才简化为产品的世界里，你坚持了下来。你一次又一次提醒我，我们所做的是关于真理和遗产，关于人类的心向另一个人类的心伸出援手，并说，‘你并不孤单。’”
戴维斯身后留下了四个孩子、八个孙子和两个曾孙。休斯顿的器乐版《我想与某人共舞（谁爱我）》随着戴维斯的棺材被抬出犹太教堂。

item.title: 直播内容：《Enola Holmes 3》、麦当娜和《律政俏佳人》前传系列《Elle》
item.description:
《Enola Holmes 3》和麦当娜的新专辑是一些即将登陆您附近设备的新电视、电影、音乐和游戏。
此外，美联社娱乐记者选出的本周值得您花时间观看的流媒体节目包括：《律政俏佳人》前传系列、拉尔夫·劳伦庆祝美国 250 岁生日的邮票收藏，以及在优质视频点播服务上推出的《痴迷》。
— 出人意料的票房持久力推迟了其在国内的上映，但库里·巴克 (Curry Barker) 的《痴迷》将于 6 月 30 日周二以溢价点播方式上映。尽管制作预算为 75 万美元，但自 5 月中旬上映以来，《焦点专题》的全球票房收入已超过 3.37 亿美元。在其中，熊（迈克尔·约翰斯顿饰）用一棵“一愿柳”来祝福他的暗恋对象（因德·纳瓦雷特饰）爱他。
— 米莉·鲍比·布朗在《艾诺拉·福尔摩斯 3》（7 月 1 日，Netflix）中回归，饰演夏洛克·福尔摩斯的妹妹。在该系列的第三部电影中，伊诺拉正在准备与图克斯伯里勋爵（路易斯·帕特里奇饰）即将举行的婚礼，这时她的弟弟夏洛克（亨利·卡维尔饰）被绑架了。
— Madge 回来了。《Confessions II》是麦当娜 2005 年深受喜爱的专辑《Confessions on a Dance Floor》的续集，于周五发行——距原版 21 年。原作被广泛认为是她重返流行音乐巅峰的作品。这张专辑广受好评，其中包括《Hung Up》、《Sorry》、《Get Together》和《Jump》等热门歌曲，是新时代俱乐部爱好者的流行舞曲迪斯科。那么，这个新时代将会带来什么呢？从之前发行的《Confessions II》曲目、呼吸感十足的《I Feel So Free》、浩室节奏的《Love Sensation》以及与 Sabrina Carpenter 合作的《Bring Your Love》来看，美好时光即将到来。
——奥斯卡奖得主让·杜雅尔丹（《艺术家》）主演了一部全新法国限定剧集《佐罗》，讲述了唐·迭戈·德拉·维加 (Don Diego de la Vega) 的故事，他是一位生活在 1821 年洛杉矶的风度翩翩、富有的贵族。这个迭戈·德拉·维加已经 20 年没有发挥出佐罗的另一个自我了，但当他出人意料地成为洛杉矶市长时，他发现自己陷入了一场与威胁他的城市的腐败作斗争中。佐罗的角色重生，德拉维加必须平衡政治、家庭和他作为义务警员的秘密角色。该节目将于 6 月 30 日星期二开始在 MHz Choice 及其 Prime Video 和 Roku 订阅频道上播放。
— 我们在新的 Prime Video 系列《Elle》中了解到，在进入哈佛法学院之前，Elle Woods 是一个快乐的青少年，住在洛杉矶阳光明媚的豪宅里，直到她父亲在……西雅图找到了一份新工作。这位活泼喜爱粉色的青少年很快发现她的新学校正处于涅槃统治的垃圾摇滚时期，甚至学校的拉拉队队员都穿着法兰绒。尽管遇到了各种挫折，艾丽最终找到了她的朋友，并开始喜欢她的新学校。新人莱克西·迈恩特里饰演年轻的艾丽，但在某些角度和某些时刻，你会发誓自己看到的是瑞茜·威瑟斯彭。第二季刚刚杀青。该片将于 7 月 1 日星期三首播。
— 您可能听说过 Ralph Lauren 为美国 250 岁生日设计了 USPS 邮票收藏。选出了十三张邮票来纪念典型的美国形象。历史频道推出的新特别节目“拉尔夫·劳伦的美国偶像”深入探讨了该项目背后的意图，其中包括以前未公开的档案材料以及对大卫·劳伦、肯·伯恩斯等人的采访。该节目将于 7 月 5 日星期日在历史频道应用程序、History.com 和 On Demand 上播出。
— 早在 Game Boy 时代，任天堂游戏机就一直吸引着那些喜欢用脚趾敲打外星人的玩家。“Rhythm Heaven Groove”延续了这一传统，挑战玩家在保持节拍的同时切蔬菜、打水果和挥舞大锤。这些只是该包中 80 个单人迷你游戏中的一小部分，其中还包括 Beatspell，这是一款节奏感十足的角色扮演冒险游戏，需要灵活的手指操作来施放咒语。您还可以召集三位朋友参加 30 场多人比赛，有些是竞争性的，有些是合作性的。7 月 2 日星期四，在 Switch 上进入最佳状态。

item.title: 康卡斯特计划通过分拆 NBCUniversal 和 Sky 来拆分为两家上市公司
item.description:
纽约（美联社）——通信巨头康卡斯特计划将自己一分为二：一个以媒体为中心的业务，包括 NBCUniversal 和 Sky 等品牌；另一个是专注于宽带和无线服务的独立公司。
康卡斯特在周一的声明中表示，拆分将使这两家公司处于更有利的位置，以追求自己的优先事项和增长。此举出台之际，通信公司仍在努力应对多年来的掐线问题，并改变了消费者现在购买订阅服务的习惯，从电话套餐到更广泛的流媒体预算。
康卡斯特董事长兼联合首席执行官布莱恩·罗伯茨(Brian Roberts)在周一的电话会议上表示：“世界的变化速度比以往任何时候都快。”他补充说，公司的技术和媒体业务“已经变得很明显”，“它们面前都面临着引人注目的机会，这些机会本质上是独特的，最好是专心致志地抓住。”
分拆完成后，两家公司都将成为自己的上市公司。康卡斯特表示，预计将在大约一年内完成这一流程，等待监管部门的批准和董事会的最终批准。
这意味着消费者不应立即感受到影响。但目前康卡斯特旗下有许多主要品牌——从互联网和无线提供商 Xfinity 到流媒体平台 Peacock、NBC News 和环球影城。分析师们正在关注这些业务未来的发展前景。
市场研究公司 Forrester 的副总裁兼研究总监迈克·普鲁克斯 (Mike Proulx) 表示：“短期内，捆绑、定价和分销可能会持续下去。”他指出，对于 NBCUniversal 来说——即将领导康卡斯特正在分拆的以媒体为中心的公司——分拆本身对其当前业务影响不大，而“更多的是与它的长期发展有关”。
Proulx 正在为这一领域的未来收购做好准备，并补充说“康卡斯特正在遵循我们已经看到的策略。”他以华纳兄弟探索公司为例，该公司去年 6 月宣布打算分拆，随后成为收购目标，并在 Netflix 和 Skydance 旗下的派拉蒙之间爆发了一场混乱的拉锯战。派拉蒙最终取得了胜利，现在距离完成以 810 亿美元收购华纳整个公司的目标又近了一步。
到目前为止，康卡斯特高管似乎否认了走向类似命运的可能性。当在周一的电话会议上被问及投资者是否应该将此次分拆视为两家公司迈向“潜在战略交易”的一步时，罗伯茨表示：“绝对不应该。”
他的联合首席执行官迈克·卡瓦纳（Mike Cavanagh）即将成为 NBCUniversal 分拆公司的首席执行官，他也表达了同样的观点。卡瓦纳重申了“建设和投资以促进增长”的计划，作为独立企业拥有更多自由。
尽管如此，Proulx 等分析师推测，即使 NBCUniversal 没有成为收购目标，“也可能是该公司进行收购。”
“就目前情况而言，传统电视正在消亡，仅凭 Peacock 还不足以与最大的流媒体服务进行大规模竞争，”普鲁克斯在电子邮件中表示。“无论怎样，NBCU 的娱乐业务在未来几年内将会有所不同。”
与其他公司一样，康卡斯特近年来将其业务重点从传统有线电视转向流媒体和其他收入来源，例如电影制片厂、主题公园以及家庭无线和互联网服务。
康卡斯特将成为两家独立的上市公司。美联社记者迈克·汉彭报道。
NBCUniversal 包括主题公园部门、环球电影和电视工作室、NBC 和 Telemundo 网络、Peacock 和 Bravo，随着分拆，欧洲媒体公司 Sky 也将成为 Cavanagh 领导的投资组合的一部分。
与此同时，总部位于费城的康卡斯特将继续为住宅和商业客户提供互联网服务。康卡斯特前首席财务官迈克尔·安吉拉基斯将在该公司分拆后担任首席执行官。
康卡斯特此前曾剥离过资产。周一的举动是在该公司正式完成对 Versant Media Group 的分拆几个月后发生的。Versant Media Group 于 2024 年 11 月首次宣布，该集团是 USA、Oxygen、E!、SYFY 和 Golf Channel 等网络以及 CNBC 和 MSNBC（现为 MS NOW）等网络的新家。电影票务平台 Fandango 和烂番茄电影评级网站也包括在内。
一旦最新的分拆完成，康卡斯特股东将同时拥有康卡斯特和 NBCUniversal 的股份。康卡斯特预计在分拆完成后一年内将保留 NBCUniversal 高达 19.9% 的股权。
周一宣布这一消息后，康卡斯特股价截至午盘交易上涨超过 6%。自 2026 年初以来，股价仍下跌超过 10%。

item.title: 韩国科技巨头将斥资 5180 亿美元建设芯片制造中心，以满足不断飙升的人工智能需求
item.description:
韩国首尔（美联社）——韩国科技巨头三星电子和 SK 海力士周一表示，他们将总共投资 800 万亿韩元（5180 亿美元）在该国西南部地区建设一个新的计算机芯片制造中心，充分利用人工智能驱动的需求激增。
总统李在明周一与两家公司的董事长一起宣布了该计划，该计划与政府扩大投资到大首尔都市区以外的努力相吻合，该地区是韩国的经济中心和半导体行业的核心。
西南地区一直是特别关注的焦点，因为它缺乏主要的工业中心，而且历史上经济发展落后。该地区长期以来一直是李明博领导的自由民主党的政治基地。
三星和 SK 海力士生产的存储芯片占全球约三分之二，两家公司表示，他们将在西南部各建两家制造工厂，扩大其在首尔南部京畿道现有制造基地的范围。
三星董事长李在镕表示，该公司的新工厂将建在西南部城市光州，专家们已经提出了几个潜在地点，包括计划搬迁的一个军事空军基地的场地。
两家公司没有具体说明西南地区的工厂何时竣工。SK 海力士董事长 Chey Tae-won 表示，该项目将是一项复杂、大规模的工程，需要“广阔的场地、充足的电力、水和熟练的工人”。他表示，SK海力士花了九年时间才在京畿道建立了主要的制造集群。Chey 表示，尽管如此，公司仍需要大幅扩建生产设施，以满足全球需求。
政府官员驳回了有关西南部是否有足够的电力和水来支持主要半导体工厂的问题。他们表示，该地区在可再生能源方面的实力将为芯片制造商带来优势，因为它们面临着使用清洁电力来源日益增长的全球压力。
由于全球对数据中心和其他人工智能基础设施的投资飙升，刺激了对存储芯片的需求，三星和 SK 海力士近几个月的利润创下历史新高。政府官员和商业专家预计，随着该技术扩展到人工智能驱动的工业机器人和自动驾驶汽车，人工智能驱动的需求将继续增加。他们表示，芯片制造商位于京畿道的现有半导体工厂可能会比预期更早达到产能。
在周一的活动中，政府官员概述了建立他们所谓的全国半导体生态系统的计划，其中东南部的现有制造中心扩大了芯片元件和材料的生产，中部忠清地区专门从事芯片封装，而数据中心则遍布全国各地。
“我们必须比任何其他国家更快地建立人工智能的核心构建模块。半导体、人工智能物理和人工智能数据中心是我们下一次跨越式发展的三大支柱，”总统说。
